title: Mississippi mud pie
subtitle: Dense, fudgy American chocolate pie
excerpt: American chocolate pie with an Oreo-crumb base, a dense brownie-style filling, and whipped cream on top. The filling sets firm at the edges and stays fudgy at the centre. Unapologetically rich.
sourceNotes: Mississippi mud pie is an American dessert documented from the mid-twentieth century, named for the dark, sticky appearance of the Mississippi River mud. It appears in American church cookbooks and regional Southern collections from the 1970s onward. The defining characteristic is the dense, fudgy filling; the Oreo-crumb base is the most common modern version but biscuit crumb or pastry bases both appear in period documentation.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the cream topping. Thaw overnight in the fridge and add fresh cream before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the whole pie a day ahead; refrigerate. Add the whipped cream just before serving. The flavour deepens overnight.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven; fan at 155°C. The filling sets at the edges while staying fudgy at the centre — the temperature is deliberately moderate.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pie or cheesecake base made from crushed biscuits mixed with melted butter, pressed into the tin and chilled or briefly baked to set. No rolling required; the result is sandy and crisp when cold.

content.text: The name refers to the dark, fudgy appearance of the set filling. The

is faster and more forgiving than pastry; the filling is a brownie batter poured directly into the unbaked base and baked together. The edge will set firm while the centre stays fudgy — this is correct, not underdone. Whipped cream on top is the classic finish.

Preheat the oven to 175°C conventional (155°C fan). Crush the biscuits to fine crumbs in a food processor or in a sealed bag with a rolling pin. Stir in the melted butter until the crumbs clump when pressed. Press firmly into the base and up the sides of a 23 cm springform tin. Refrigerate while you make the filling.

Melt the chocolate and butter together in a saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat. Whisk in the sugar, then beat in the eggs one at a time. Sift in the flour, cocoa, and salt and fold until combined and smooth.

Pour the filling over the crumb base. Bake for 30–35 minutes: the edges should be set and pulling away slightly from the tin, the centre should still wobble when gently shaken. A skewer inserted 3 cm from the edge will come out clean; one in the centre will still be wet. This is correct. Cool completely in the tin, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours. Run a knife around the edge and release the springform. Pile whipped cream on top and serve cold.
